{{Quote|<nowiki></nowiki>''[Andov]'' Syn is best known for an incident on the planet Adim. A mining colony beset by pirates had hired six mercenaries to protect them. The pirates, in turn, hired Syn, who singlehandedly eliminated all of the town's guardians.|Airen Cracken, ''Wanted by Cracken''|<ref name="WbC" />}}
'''Adim''' was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="WbC" /> located in the [[Adim system/Legends|Adim system]], a part of the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim's]] [[Adari sector/Legends|Adari sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was the site of a [[City/Legends|town]] that functioned as a [[Adim mining colony|mining colony]].<ref name="WbC" />

At some point by [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]],<ref name="Date">[[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']] is dated to three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. Since ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates that conflict to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the placement of the sourcebook therefore translates to [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]].</ref> in response to attacks by a group of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]], the [[Mining/Legends|mining]] [[Colonist/Legends|colony]] hired six [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] for protection. The pirates, however, hired the [[Kerestian/Legends|Kerestian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Andov Syn]], who proceeded to singlehandedly wipe out the mercenaries. The incident on Adim eventually cemented Syn's reputation as a [[Death/Legends|deadly]] [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]]. The [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] subsequently noted the incident on Adim in ''[[Wanted by Cracken]]'', a [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] that served as a compilation of reports on various threats to the New Republic.<ref name="WbC" />

==Behind the scenes==
Adim was first mentioned in the [[1993]] sourcebook [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']], written by [[Louis J. Prosperi]] for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="WbC" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Adim [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet Adim, in grid square M-9.<ref name="TEA" />
